reviews.title: Space bar was too big:( still cute tho
reviews.text: The keys are so cute! I love it! The only downside was that the space bar was too big for my keyboard!! It was also too big on my brothers keyboard, so that’s a little weird. So sad about it too since that was the main reason why I got it:(

reviews.title: Really cheap look and feel
reviews.text:
These are really cheap. I know you might be thinking, you get what you pay for and these are cheaply priced. However, at the end of the day, keycaps are just plastic and the good ones are just waaay over priced.

First thing first, the actual color differs from the picture. The picture has a nice looking matcha green, the actual product is a little darker green and no where as vibrant. Also, some of the legends on the keycaps are kind of blurry. I am noticing the blurriness on all the non-alpha caps. You can tell these are really cheap and they look pretty bad.

title: Doohoeek Updated Retro Typewriter Keyboard with White Lighting, Muti-Device Multi-OS Bluetooth & Wired Mechanical Keyboard for Laptops, Vintage Keyboard with Cradle for Mac OS/iOS/Windows/Android
features: New Version for More Flexibility: The 2022 updated typewriter keyboard is compatible with more operating system (MacOS and iOS) and also simplify the connectivity and switch process with easy connect switch button.

Retro Typewriter Style Design: The wood finished surface, retro round keys and typewriter-like looks provide good balance between functional keyboard and vintage decoration. Best decors and functional keyboard for your office or home setups.

Multi-OS with Easy Switch Button: The keyboard is compatible with Mac OS, iOS, Windows and Android operating system and only simply tap on the switch button at the top right corner to change the configiration.

Pair up to 4 Devices with Seamless Switch: The keyboard supports 3 Bluetooth and 1 wired connectivity. Only simply press switch button at the top right corner to switch between Bluetooth and wired mode. (For more details about Bluetooth connectivity please scroll down to product description)
Responsive and Fluid Keystrokes for Heavy Users: All key anti-ghosting and lifespan of over 50 million clicks provide fluid and responsive typing experience for writers, gamers, coders, etc.

Beautiful White Lighting Effects: The various lighting effects allow you to type day or night while decorating your desktop setups . Only need to fiddle with the “return” bar and left-hand knob to change lighting effects and its brightness.

Long Lasting Battery: With 2000mah rechargeable battery only about 6 hours charging the keyboard has 16-hour continuouse working time (backlighting on). Note: Working time may vary based on use and computer conditions.

What You Get: 1 x Retro Typewriter Keyboard, 2 x Blue Switch, 1 x Metal Switch Puller, 1 x USB-C Cable for Charging/Connection, 1 x User Manual; 12-month warranty & a ready support team for any issues.

reviews.title: LOVE IT
reviews.text: I absolutely 💯 ♥️ love this keyboard! It reminds me of typing class in high school. If you want nostalgia this is your product. It does make a very satisfying loud clicking when typing so do not buy if you need something quiet. Now my sister wants one too!

reviews.title: I am in love
reviews.text: I never thought I can fall in love with a keyboard! This keyboard is esthetically beautiful. I love this clicking sound…not quite like the real typewriter but kinda close:)  Light effects make typing even more fun

reviews.title: Beautiful keyboard
reviews.text: The keyboard is absolutely unique and stunning, I just received it yesterday so I have not typed on it very much but it seems pretty heavy duty and the back lighting is awesome especially with bad eyesight, The return arm regulates the lights to make it very smooth to adjust, The typewriter knobs actually do the volume on your computer so all in all I do love the keyboard, The wireless feature actually was pretty easy to set up just read the instructions carefully!  I have never spent more than $40 on a keyboard and wireless mouse but I have to tell you well worth it.

reviews.title: nice build. aesthetically interesting looking.
reviews.text: I tried to return this thinking it has connectivity issues but troubleshot my way to realize it was OS messin' with me.  Glad I gave it a another whirl because it's really nice!  one thing it does do is the lower keys next to the space button are all the same height and my fat lower palm always hits them and sends me to the beginning of the sentence I'm writing.  Annoying and somewhat dangerous for my line of work but I solved it by putting elevation risers at the front raising it approximately another 8-10 degrees.  Would have been nice if it had elevation standoffs as standard equipment.  bluetooth on this is fantastic and responsive.

title: Azio Retro Classic Bluetooth (Elwood) - Wireless/USB Wired Walnut Wood Vintage Backlit Mechanical Keyboard for PC/Mac, Walnut Wood (MK-RETRO-W-01B-US) & RM-RCM-L-03 Retro Classic Mouse (Artisan)
description: Azio Retro Classic Bluetooth (Elwood) - Luxury Vintage Backlit Mechanical Keyboard, Metallic Brown/Grey (MK-RETRO-W-01B-US)Inspired by vintage typewriters and crafted with ultramodern features, the Azio Retro Classic is the perfect amalgam of past, present and future. The retro classic is a backlit mechanical keyboard with round keycaps that evokes nostalgia of yesteryear complemented with a durable aluminum frame and a sumptuous genuine leather or walnut surface.Azio RM-RCM-L-03 Retro Classic Mouse (Artisan)The retro classic mouse (RCM) stays true to the minimalist philosophy in order to accentuate its ambidextrous design. With its modern features and ability to work on virtually any surface, The RCM is an essential companion for your retro classic & compact keyboard.
features: Product 1: A classic reborn: design originated by vintage typewriters and crafted with ultramodern features, the Azio Retro Classic is the perfect amalgam of past, present, and future.
Product 1: Genuine & luxurious: Genuine leather or wood top plate. Leather reflects taste, craftsmanship, and exclusiveness while emitting a unique charm that inspires.
Product 1: Tactile. Clicky. Backlit: the backlit mechanical keys are tuned to be tactile and "clicky", reminiscent of vintage typewriters.
Product 1: Forged. Polished. Plated: The keyboard frame is forged with aluminum alloy and plated into a beautiful chrome/Satin finish to accompany the leather top plate.
Product 2: GENUINE LEATHER SURFACE - Utilizing top-grain chrome-tanned leather to provide a pleasant touch, each leather piece is precision-cut, molded in, and hand-trimmed to perfection.

Product 2: EXQUISITE CRAFTMANSHIP - Immaculate attention to every detail - Genuine leather top cover, textured aluminum wheel and alloy frame with precision bolts. The scroll wheel design starts with a high-grade aluminum rod that is knurled into a diamond-shaped pattern and completed with chamfered edges on a precision CNC lathe. It is then anodized to enhance aesthetics and durability.
Product 2: TRACKS Virtually ANYWHERE - The PixArt PAW3805 sensor is designed to track flawlessly on virtually any surface, including glass and other high-gloss materials.

Product 2: DUAL USB-RF & BLUETOOTH - Interfaces Connect wirelessly with Bluetooth or RF via USB receiver. Works on Bluetooth enabled PC's natively but also includes a USB RF receiver for computers without Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Check Your Computer First
reviews.text:
I sure hated to send this beauty back to Amazon, but I gave it a fair trial and I'm just not satisfied, and here's why.
It's a beautiful piece of machinery/artwork and weighs in at about 10 lbs. The keyboard glows in such a way that it will illuminate your work area in a dark room, and you can adjust the brightness up or down or off.
No, my complaint is with the speed at which it reacts. This keyboard has to keep up with you, possibly in order to mimic an old fashioned Royal or Underwood. I'm used to a $30 Logitech wireless that I can wag to the kitchen with my laptop. I don't like to use the laptop keyboard and scroll pad, I like a separate keyboard, it's faster for me. I find myself correcting errors constantly, and it slows me down, but I'm no stenographer either.
This Azio is Bluetooth, but not all computers support it. My 6 year old Toshiba doesn't, so I have to use the USB connection cord and block a precious port full time.
What a shame, it sure is elegant, and a lot of folks won't care about the perceived lag time.
The star rating posed a problem for me because I'm trying to be honest about my needs and I realize another might be perfectly satisfied.
This brute is expensive, and I'm not inclined to spend north of $700 for a new computer when I don't need one, just to support it.

reviews.title: Good keyboard with one minor catch
reviews.text: Very good keyboard, but it is heavier than I thought.  The height is not easier adjusted so I have to leave my drop down keyboard tray stuck out.

reviews.title: Great Keyboard - Bad Reliability Plus Non-Existent Support
reviews.text: Ordered this roughly a year ago. Started having problems with the mouse within a few months, with the mouse not holding a charge. Got the product replaced under warranty. Replacement mouse never would connect via the USB dongle consistently. 10 months in or so, my keyboard started acting up and, despite being plugged in via USB-C on my desk, continually shows out of power / red light. When I move it to Bluetooth it strangely works, so it can't be out of power. Reached out to Azio support and got crickets. Now have a nearly $200 set of pretty chrome and leather and plastic paperweight. Looks great, fabulous when it work. Sadly that's not very consistently or long. I've got cheap keyboards that lasted multiple years easily.

reviews.title: Not Bad
reviews.text: The keyboard and mouse was packed well for the shipping and arrived in great shape.  Very tactile feel and old school look.

reviews.title: Incorrect Description Information - Wrong item
reviews.text: First off it is very cute and I loved it, but the description says wireless/wired, giving the indication that it can be used wireless and it cannot.  This is a lot of money for a keyboard that has to be plugged in.  Also, I can’t tell how to get in touch with the seller so I can get confirmation.  RETURNING.

title: HONKID Esc Keycap for Mechanical Gaming Keyboard Switches, Custom Keycaps for Esc and Tab Key with Magnetic Light Macintosh Retro Style Design (White)
features: TRIBUTE TO THE CLASSICS: The retro computer keycaps is in honor of the the classic 1984 Macintosh computers, the unique retro styling of ESC keycap and Tab keycap give you a new experience

COMPATIBILITY: Uinversal mechanical keyboard Cross interface, compatible with most MX switches as cherry MX, kailh, outemu, gateron, ect. Our custom keycaps fit most keyboards especially 60% mechanical gaming keyboard, the keycaps include ESC keycap and Tab keycap

LIGHT-TRANSMISSION DESIGN: Unique light guide design, the light transmission is brighter and more uniform. If your keyboard has a backlight function, then your light will be delivered through the keycap card, and the effect is perfect. Five classic card patterns, which can be changed at will

MAGNETIC DESIGN: Both ESC and Tab keycap have magnetic, simple and stable.You can attach the mini keyboard to any keycap, card panel of the keycap also uses the magnetic function, which makes it very convenient and fast to replace the translucent cards
DELICATE EXQUISITE: You will feels better and more delicate and comfortable,The delicate matte texture and the details of the back are enough to make you miss the first generation of MAC

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

reviews.title: Shine through is very disappointing
reviews.text: I got these primarily because I liked the orange and black color scheme and the advertised shine through features but the backlit brightness is only fair to poor on these (I'm being generous giving it 3 stars, I think).  Even the cheap $6.00 keycap set ($0.38 after discounts) with a similar color scheme I got from aliexpress is brighter than these.  However, since I need those 1u-sized right Alt, FN and Ctrl keycaps for my Royal Kludge RK96 90% keyboard, I'm not returning therm, though I basically paid $20 for those 3 keycaps :/

title: YUNZII AKKO CS Switch, 3 Pins for DIY MX Mechanical Keyboard, 45 pcs (Ocean Blue Switch)
features: 【About AKKO CS Switches】 AKKO Custom Series (CS) switches are the newly designed 3-pin switches for customers who have specific typing and gaming needs. Each package has 45 switches. Six types of switches are available and no matter which kind of typing feeling you want, we cover it all.

【Compatibility】AKKO CS switches are designed with a general MX structure so that they can fit most keycaps sets. The switch has 3 pins but also fit in all 3 pins and 5 pins keyboards. Based on the strong compatibility, you can easily change the switch and customize your mechanical keyboard.
【Built-in LED Slot 】The AKKO CS switches are equipped with a north-facing LED slot for LED modification. It is also compatible with SMD LED, provides more fun and options for keyboard enthusiasts.

【Durable Lifespan & Improved Performance】With a lifespan of approximately 50 million times of keystrokes, you are assured of high quality and a long lifespan. The CS switches feature a spiral spring design that can make the typing more comfortable.

【Ocean Blue Tactile Switch】The tactile bump of the Ocean Blue switch is at 0.55mm and it ends at 1.88mm, which gives long-continued feedback. Different from the blue and brown switch, the Ocean blue switch has a medium tactile position that can give you an excellent experience in typing and gaming.

reviews.title: Very good for the price
reviews.text: I'm not a mechanical keyboard connoisseur, but needed to replace the switches on my cheap Redragon keyboard to get rid of the loud clicking noise. The original switches were blue switch clones and the clicks will wake up the neighbors. These are cheap enough that I decided to give them a try. They are pretty good in terms of touch and sound, from my perspective and my use. Will definitely resort to these again in the future if I need to replace keyboard switches, especially for budget keyboards.

reviews.title: Great budget keycaps
reviews.text: Please don’t mind my cat lol she’s modeling the keyboard. Really great budget keycaps, super pretty color. I use aqua king switches and the sound with the keycaps is pretty good. Really like and enjoy them. P.S. the yellow keycaps ARE NOT part of the set, they are from another keycap set. I thought it would compliment the purple nicely :)

reviews.title: Nice keycaps, but holy crap they're snug
reviews.text:
These keycaps are probably best suited for soldered switches. If you're on a hot swap board, get ready to separate every key from the cap as you'll be pulling the entire assembly out EVERY TIME.

Other than my tolerance/keycap ripping issues these are solid and have a good sound indicative of a good pbt material.

reviews.title: Great keycap set, just wish they we're backlight compatible.
reviews.text: That's my fault, I always assume keycaps are backlight compatible but they feel awesome, love the satin touch, they look great, and the legend is super bright and clear. If you're not specifically looking for a keycap set to use with a backlit keyboard, this one looks great.

reviews.title: Looks great, types great
reviews.text:
The caps are thick and wide so typing on them is a pleasure. I also love the oriental graphics of ocean waves and whales. The only gripe would be the packaging box is a bit robust in need of better designs and material. Otherwise, this is a great product. Oh also better font selection would be better too, especially the non-capitalized letters on the pause vs page up vs page down vs delete vs insert buttons. They seem inconsistent.

I would pay an extra $5 if this keycap set supports 65% or 68% design.

reviews.title: You get what you pay for
reviews.text:
Overall it's a decent set and a good value. However, I noticed a number of stains on the keys, incorrect legends (the n in the End key), reversed prints (colon/semicolon and apostrophe/quotation were swapped), and misaligned legends (the F9 is not in line with the other F keys).

I would probably not buy from this seller or manufacturer again due to this.

reviews.title: Not great
reviews.text: My son got it for gaming and we returned it after one day because the keys were sticking. That’s clearly not ideal for a gamer.

reviews.title: ULTRA Smooth, ZERO scratch and SAVES your RGBs, what else do you want?
reviews.text: These are so smooth you wont even believe it until you put it up to your ear and will hear nothing going on on the inside of the switch. After trying the Oil Kings and being super impressed, I said fine lets try the CJ Dark Blue Lubed switch and see what the fuss is about and I am glad I did. It is the end game switch. Stop the search right here. These are dead smooth a 10 out of 10, OK are about 7-10 and the Gateron Red Pro are somewhere like 5-10. They are all hard to tell and distinguish between the smoothness but once you test them out to your ear/finger you will be able to tell. I don't believe you need to lube these because well, they come lubed and since they are so smooth I don't see a need. Also the benefit with this switch is that this CJ Dark Blue comes with a slot for the LED to shine through just fine, so you will save your RGB as I have and I am glad.

Used this for my Switch Couture Electrotype60 amazing PCB and pretty easy to solder, especially for an amateur solderer like me, definitely will be buying another one when I have another case to build. Fit perfectly, No problems soldering, and definitely came out perfect out of the box, though I don't know how to brighten up the LED nor change the modes, I'm completely fine with the RGB cycle. I'm Typing on my new keyboard as we speak and it works perfectly, no delays, no nothing, just perfect!!!!

(I can admit I'm kinda too last to do VIA so I'm just working with it lol)

title: Akko Pink Keycaps Set, Clear Translucent 155-Key ASA Profile Keys with Mac Keycaps Set for Mechanical Keyboard
features: TRANSPARENT KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Clear keycap sets! Made by polycarbonate, the keycap is solid and translucent so that your switches and RGB array can shine through. Characters on the ASA Clear keycaps are processed through UV printing with wear-resistant and scratch-resistant tests, and are not easily wiped off.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 155-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

MAC KEYCAPS & NOVELTY KEYS – The keycap set is designed for both Windows and Mac users, where Mac modifiers CMD/OPT are offered in different sizes. The transparent keycap set also features extra cute animal themed novelty keys for decoration.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: Impressed
reviews.text: Very good packaging and acceptable quality

reviews.title: Unique keys look cool with RGB. Some drawbacks.
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Beautifully and conveniently packaged and organized. Caps come on three trays with plastic film on them so that nothing will have shifted in transit. It's very easy to replace your caps and keep your old set save and organized in the trays, too. Simple to find any specialized keys that you may need in the trays.
- Print is easy to see and seems durable/high contrast. No funky fonts that are hard to read.
- Looks awesome with RGB.

Cons:

- There are no tactile nubs on the F and J keys to indicate homerow. As a touch typist, this was almost a dealbreaker.
- Color is way off from the images in the description. The images in the description show a clear white color to the keycaps. Along with the "clear/translucent" descriptors, I believed I was going to get more of a white/iced translucent look. Nope. The caps are basically colorless clear. They are smooth and glossy with no texture. I left some of the OG white keys on my keyboard to show the color clearly side by side. Not the look I was expecting from the images and description.

Good to know:

- I've never used ASA profile before, and generally prefer XDA, so these are a little taller than I'm used to on XDA, but more rounded (and, I think, more comfortable, than OEM. I had some lousy stock OEM caps on my old keyboard, and I do feel these caps are an improvement. They are sufficiently dense, and give me a slightly deeper, more muted sound from my keypresses with my brown switches, which I like, compared to the more plasticky/hollow caps that were original to my board.
- Despite having a glossy finish, I don't find these keys to be slippery--the slight curvature leads your fingertips toward the center of the keys easily and they're comfortable to use.
- I really hate that there are no homerow nubs. Who skips that? Come on.

reviews.title: No tactile bumps on F and J, amazing for backlighting
reviews.text:
I have the “White” color option in this set of keycaps from Akko. White is a bit misleading, the color is actually glossy and nearly translucent. This is actually what I wanted, I was looking all over for nearly clear keycaps, but I did want to point that out.

If you like maximum backlighting, this might be the set for you. I was using white pudding caps before, and I thought they let a lot of light shine through. The difference is night and day. These Akko caps show backlighting on all sides and the top of the cap. I’ve included a picture of a white pudding cap surrounded by the clear Akkos to show the difference.

I’m using the keycaps with Akko Vintage White (MX) switches. The keycaps fit well, but they took a bit of force to fit on the switches.

I am disappointed the F and J keys do not have ridges or bumps. I’m a touch typist and using an ergo board with 56 keys, I rely on those ridges to help me orient my hands. This set has so much variety that I wish Akko had included doubles of F and J or even blanks with the ridges.

Speaking of keycap variety: the set has some great 1u options! My current keyboard is an Iris Rev 6, all keys are 1u size. But most keycap sets don’t have 1u keycaps for Shift, Control, Command/Windows, and so on. This set does, which is a big help. I also like the cute animal keycaps, they add some fun to my board.

I believe the ASA height is original to Akko. They have a helpful height chart on their site, comparing ASA to OEM, JDA, OSA, and Cherry. I was using OEM before, and switching to ASA and the glossy feel of the keycaps took about two days of adjustment.

title: EPOMAKER Macaron 1.8m Coiled Type-C to USB A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Cable with Detachable Aviator Connector for Gaming Keyboard/Tablet/Smart Phone (Purple)
features: 【Epomaker Macaron Cable For Keyboards and More】 To provide more options of cables, Epomaker custom Macaron cable is designed to match your keyboard, no matter what type of setup you’re working with. Get it to refresh your keyboard right away!

reviews.title: It looks so much cooler!
reviews.text: These are way cooler than the standard cords! Just look at them guys.

reviews.title: Beautiful cable, working perfectly
reviews.text: I've wanted a coiled cable for a while but they're all so expensive. Found this epomaker one and I've bought products from epomaker before and haven't had any issues, and since this cable was only $20 I figured I could risk it. I was even happier when I saw they had a lavender option, most places just have light pink but not lavender. This cable matches my keycaps perfectly. The coiled part lays flat right out of the box (I've seen a few from other companies where the coiled part kinda curls up, which I didnt want), the cable feels nice and sturdy and good quality, and the metal connector also feels nice and heavy. I also like that this is a woven cable and not just plastic/rubber wrapped. Had no issues with the keyboard working after plugging in the cable. Absolutely worth the $20, I might buy the light pink one to match my other keyboards.

reviews.title: Kawaii !!
reviews.text: Comes with all the keys caps you need, love how smooth it feels. You may need to dim your keyboard brightness, found the light through the clear key caps can hurt your eyes after a while.

reviews.title: Exceptionally perfect feel EXCEPT no way to find your position!
reviews.text:
I wanted to love these key caps. They look great! They feel superb and their smooth texture is exactly what I wanted.

However, two major flaws: I didn't even know this was a thing to consider, but they don't have bumps on the positioning keys "F" and "J". So if you type a lot and remove your hands from the keys, you need to look down every time to find your position. This interrupts my workflow as a transcriptionist quite a bit. The other disadvantage is that the smooth feel of the material also makes it impossible to keep skin oil, snack residues, etc. from being left behind on these caps, making them somewhat difficult to grip after the first half hour.

reviews.title: Really nice visual upgrade
reviews.text:
I wanted to upgrade my keyboard’s appearance, and after much Googling I settled on this product. I have a full size 104 key keyboard, and looking at keycaps on various websites makes it seem kinda complicated to know which set I’d need for my specific keyboard (Das Keyboard 4). Some Ctrl keys are wider than others, Spacebars shorter than others, etc. and what I liked about this set is that it includes different length keys and you just use the one that fits your keyboard and ignore the rest.

This is my first set of keycaps so I can’t really speak with any knowledge to the quality of the keys, but I’m really happy with how they look. They feel similar to the ones that came with my keyboard but look muuuuch better.

reviews.title: Fantastic keycap set
reviews.text:
They finally arrived! I plan on putting these keycaps on a 75% keyboard that won't arrive for another couple of months so in the pictures, I only put it on part of my keyboard to test them out. I want to "preserve" that new keycap feeling later. :)

These keycaps are fairly thick and have the standard PBT texture. The doubleshot legends are quite clear and none of the spacebars are warped! This is a new profile I've never typed on and I can't find much information on the web about it so I included a lot of pictures. I compared them in the pictures to my Ducky keyboard with doubleshot ABS. They actually aren't too different from OEM keycap profile (from the Ducky) height but some of the rows are higher. They're much more shapely and the F and J keys have a deeper dish. Compared to DSA keycaps, they don't seem to be quite as deep dishey overall which I actually prefer.
The keycaps fit snug on cherry mx brown stems, more so than my Ducky keycaps. They aren't hard to put on though. Some of the stems have a small part of plastic missing from the MX cross area but it's only a handful of the keycaps and it didn't effect the fit. I've included photos of this as well.
Overall they're great keycaps that I won't be using yet until my keyboard for them arrives.

title: 141 Keys Botanical Keycaps Cherry Profile PBT Dye Sub Keycap Set Suitable for 61/64/68/84/87/96/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches Mechanical Gaming Keyboard
features: 【PBT Keycaps】These 141 cherry profile keycaps are made of thick PBT material, with a matte texture and anti-grease coating, which can provide good durability and typing.

【Cherry Profile】This set of pbt keycaps uses the cherry height, which is higher than the XDA profile. It's ergonomically designed, so you will not feel tired even if you type or play games for a long time.
【Rugged and Durable】The thickness of these keycaps are about 1.4~1.5mm, so the keycaps are very durable. The thick PBT material will effectively extend the service life of the keycaps.

【Wide Selection】These keycaps are suitable for most keyboards, such as 61/64/68/84/87/96/104/108 cherry mx switches mechanical Keyboards.If you are not sure whether this keycaps set is fit for your keyboard, please consult customer service.
【Warm Tips】This product is a set of keycaps, not a keyboard. If the keycaps are missing or cracked, don't worry, we'll send you new replacement keys.

reviews.title: OMG great keycap set!
reviews.text:
I have been looking for these sets for the longest time, outside of GB and these guys have made a solid replica. i am so impressed with the quality and feel of these Botanical keycap sets that the premium sets can remain on the shelf. They look so nice and very very close and clean to the originals.

the only way you can tell that they are not GMK is by looking at the bottom. The dye-sub is a little weak in the finish but you never look under the hood.

If you like the greenery landscape or the color great fanatic this is the best set. It's super clean, subtle, and sounds great! I have this set on my KBD67 Lite and it sounds so much better than my ABS keycaps i paid $80 for. The texture is also very nice and I seem to be keen on this rough like texture than the ABS smoother feel. It kind of is matte if that makes sense. The legends on all the alphas are uniform, no thickness issues. The ,.? and other modifiers might have a thinner font but negligible.

I can't say enough what a great first impression these sets have given me and if they produce an "Oblivian" replica i'm jumping on it as well. Pictures to come, after I pickup my desk from all the loose keycaps.

reviews.title: Nice looking and a great add for keyboard enthusiasts!
reviews.text:

Only ding I would give it is that if the keyboard needs to be to the left the cable off the coils is on top and does not look as nice. If the cable is oriented to the right, it looks better. I don't have any major issues here, but it is something that shoudl be in the product description maybe.

Overall, really nice and glad I bought it!

title: ikbc W200 Wireless Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switch for Windows and Mac OS, Enables Media Key and LED Indicator (2.4G Dongle, USB 2.0, PBT Double Shot 87 Keycaps, Black Color, ANSI/US)
features: ▼ Mechanical keyboard with Cherry MX Red Switches (Linear): A linear, lighter but does not have tactile bump and audible click found comparing with Cherry MX Blue switches. Cherry MX Red is ideal for gaming where the low articulation force and linear path allow for quick successive key presses. Typing with Cherry MX Red switches is similar to browns, though it does lack tactile feedback and It is always the best preference for gamers.
▼ A durable computer keyboard with PBT Double Shot keycaps for Ergonomic Keycap Shapes designed to provide you with durable, long lasting and comfortable typing experience.

▼ Plug-And-Play Simplicity. : Setup takes seconds—you simply plug the wireless receiver into a USB port and start using your keyboard right away without software. 100% Anti-ghosting with 6-key Rollover and convenient FN Multimedia Shortcuts Keys.

▼ Other Features : Original Cherry MX Switch-Each Reliable Switches Rated for 50 million Typing Times. Using 2.4Ghz Wireless can Connectivity-Up to 6 Month Long Standby Time. Product structure include the Smart Power Indicator Light and 2 Adjustable Feetstand Height.
▼ What You Can Get: iKBC Cherry MX Red switch mechanical keyboard x1, 2.4G wireless dongle x1 (equipped inside battery area), AAA batteries x2, dust cover x1, key puller x1, user manual x1.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text:
It meets all my needs. I got it to replace a wireless Velocifire keyboard whose internal battery was dying. I had to plug my old one in everyday as I couldn’t see the key labels without the backlight, and it’s built in battery life decreased from 5 to 1 days over a couple years. So it was essentially wired, while I prefer the wireless aesthetic.

This one avoids the backlight killing the battery life as well as internal battery degradation. I can see the key labels fine without a backlight, and it takes replaceable batteries. Battery life should be months without the backlight, and it even has a low battery warning light. No cord to deal with.

Seems well built, heavy, and doesn’t slide on the desk. Nice price point. Easy to set up. It even has multiple tilt angle (height) options, although I like it flat. TKL is great to minimize the distance to reach the mouse, improving ergonomics.

I went from brown to red switches as that’s all that was in stock, and it was an easy change, although I could tell a slight difference initially.

reviews.title: Quite Loud
reviews.text:
If you can't stand the sound of a clacking keyboard, this isn't for you. It can get quite loud, especially when you're typing fairly quickly. This is probably better for techies who like the intense sound and the tactile key interface. The lighting is also nice. I can recommend this keyboard for those who either live or work alone.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided helps make your decision a little easier.

reviews.title: A No-Cable Classic For Writers
reviews.text:
I am not lying: This is hands down the best keyboard for writing I have used in the last twenty years. I liked the older acrylic Apple keyboard and I own a mechanical keyboard with click-switches (blue), but this one makes touch typing so much more comfortable.
I wasn’t sure if I would like the tactile keys without click, because I actually like the click. They are just very loud. Logitech’s K845 (with Cherry MX red) is a great keyboard as well, but louder and has a cable.
I wish this keyboard would be backlit though. Black keyboards in front of big screens need some sort of illumination. I got one of these USB LED lights that I plugged behind my monitor, that half-solves the problem by providing some backlight.
Still it’s awesome to write on it. It’s focus is to be a mechanical keyboard. So much that it seems that it skipped the product design. It looks like an OEM-Keyboard from 1995. That’s in a way uncompromisingly functional, but next to a modern flat butterfly keyboard it looks like someone parked an old Volvo next to a Miata.
The quality is good. The keycaps are heavy and lettering sharp. It might be confusing for some that the ‘upper’ characters are printed next to the main set in the same size.
Battery life seems not to be a problem. I am pretty much writing all day, and I’m still on the first set of batteries that came in the package.
PROS AND CONS
+ Precise keyboard for touch-typing
+ Quiet enough for shared work areas
+ Height adjustable
+ Great quality
+ Long Battery life
+ Connects via dongle, not Bluetooth (yes, for me that’s a pro. Bluetooth sucks)
+ Compact with no number pad

- Chunky design
- No backlight
- Very heavy
- Vague keycap lettering
- No number pad (*conflict*)
I can recommend this keyboard to anyone who is typing over a long amount of times. It takes a while to get used to, but once adjusted it’s less tiring than modern butterfly or rubber cap keyboards, because the keys don’t bottom out that easily, and feel better to the fingertips. I took a few photos. I hope it helps to make a decision.

reviews.title: Silent Cherry Red Switches Work Well With Family Room Computer
reviews.text:
While the cherry MX red keys are often used for gaming, I am reviewing this as a typing keyboard in a family room, where the loud clackity clack of a keyboard is unwelcome. And I find this keyboard to be a great solution for such an environment.

This keyboard paired so effortlessly with my Mac that I wasn’t even aware it happened. I checked the keys for their mapping, and found them to be as one would expect for a Windows keyboard—just the usual issue with the non-Apple standard keys near the Shift key.

It is not “full-size,” but 80% size, so it is somewhat smaller, but not so much so that you fee cramped.

They keys are as silent as advertised. And while an old-school touch typist might miss the “click” feedback of a mechanical keyboard, these keys have decent travel, so if you are typing, you will have little doubt that you have actually engaged the key. (I can’t talk to gaming with these.)

I have had no trouble with the bluetooth connection, nor the responsiveness while typing.

If I have any reliability issues, I will be sure to revisit this review as necessary.

reviews.title: No complaints
reviews.text: No complaints, Would buy again.
